arts.ttl: Fulham v Crystal Palace: FA Cup quarter-final – live
arts.sum: FA Cup quarter-final news from the 12.15pm GMT kick-off Live scores | Read the latest Football Daily | Mail Dominic It’s FA Cup quarter-final weekend, with the first of these four slightly unlikely ties – Manchester City apart – coming in the form of a London derby at Craven Cottage. Fulham and Crystal Palace are both chasing history in this competition this season, having never lifted the cup. Palace came close nearly a decade ago in that 2016 final against Manchester United, (Alan Pardew’s dance, Jesse Lingard’s goal and all that) while it’s been 50 years since Fulham last got all the way to a Wembley showpiece. One of them is guaranteed a semi-final trip to north London at least. Jean-Philippe Mateta looks set to make his return after suffering a nasty head injury in Palace’s previous FA Cup game against Millwall, with both teams fortunate enough to have come through the international break without a long list of absentees. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Live Blog: Fulham v Crystal Palace
arts.sum: Crystal Palace are battling for a place in the FA Cup semi-finals when they take on Fulham at Craven Cottage this afternoon (12:15 GMT) – follow live updates from West London below!

arts.ttl: Fulham vs Crystal Palace: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Two London clubs face off in the first of an intriguing set of FA Cup quarter-finals this weekend as Fulham take on Crystal Palace at Craven Cottage. Palace enter the cup tie as one of the Premier League's form teams, but they're six points behind Fulham, having played one game fewer.
